question: What is the primary purpose of abstraction in Object-Oriented Programming?
options: To hide complex implementation details and show only essential features
To make code run faster by removing unnecessary parts
To allow multiple classes to share the same methods
To create graphical user interfaces more easily
explanation: Abstraction simplifies complex systems by hiding implementation details and exposing only the necessary functionality to the user.

question: In Java, which keyword is used to declare an abstract class?
options: abstract
interface
virtual
abstraction
explanation: The 'abstract' keyword is used to declare an abstract class in Java, which cannot be instantiated and may contain abstract methods.

question: Which of the following statements about abstract methods in Java is correct?
options: Abstract methods have no body and must be overridden by subclasses
Abstract methods can have a default implementation in the abstract class
Abstract methods are declared with the 'virtual' keyword
Abstract methods can be declared in any concrete class
explanation: Abstract methods are declared without an implementation (no body) using the abstract keyword and must be implemented by any concrete subclass.

question: What is the key difference between an abstract class and an interface in Java?
options: Abstract classes can have implemented methods and state, while interfaces only declare contracts
Interfaces can have constructors, while abstract classes cannot
Abstract classes support multiple inheritance, while interfaces do not
Interfaces can have instance fields, while abstract classes cannot
explanation: Abstract classes can contain both abstract and concrete methods with implementation and state (fields), while interfaces primarily declare method signatures (contracts) without implementation (until default methods were introduced).

options: The code will not compile because Car doesn't implement the move() method
The code will compile successfully even without move() implementation
The code will not compile because abstract classes cannot have constructors
The code will not compile because abstract classes cannot have concrete methods like displaySpeed()
explanation: The code will not compile because Car, being a concrete class, must provide an implementation for all abstract methods inherited from the abstract Vehicle class, including the move() method.
